prompt:
Use case: stylized-concept
Asset type: project-bound RPG gaslamp-industrial atlas map, first batch pass, landscape orientation
Primary request: Blades in the Dark / Doskvol inspired industrial-gothic world map, dual-continent terrain type.
Scene/backdrop: overhead birds-eye atlas view with two dark industrial-gothic landmasses separated by uneven black seas and narrow straits.
Subject: two asymmetric continents divided by a diagonal S-shaped and L-bending Void Sea corridor, not a simple vertical split. Include island-studded straits, inland black seas, soot-black gulfs, capes, broken industrial coasts, canal deltas, jagged peninsulas, Doskvol-like canal districts, lightning rail lines, leaky industrial waterways, walled cities, gang territory domains marked by non-text symbols, deathlands beyond city walls, ghost-lit sectors, electroplasmic lightning towers, smuggling docks, leviathan ports, fog marshes, coal hills, tundra, forests, mountains, wetlands, and ash plains.
Style/medium: Doskvol city district map expanded to continental scale, gothic industrial zones, criminal faction territories marked by non-text sigils, gaslamp fantasy atlas, highly detailed illustrated RPG sourcebook cartography.
Composition/framing: wide 16:9 landscape map, both continents fully visible, diagonal/S/L-shaped sea separation, generous black ocean border, top-down map clarity.
Lighting/mood: industrial gothic, perpetual supernatural darkness, smog and fog, flickering gas lamps, electroplasmic blue lightning, faction clocks ticking toward war, hungry ghosts beyond the towers.
Color palette: charcoal black, tarnished brass, cold blue electroplasm, sickly green fog, soot grey, muted brick red, oil-black ocean.
Materials/textures: blackened parchment, etched coastlines, gaslight glow, soot stains, canal-line geometry, ghostly blue technical overlays.
Constraints: no text labels, no letters, no readable words, no legend, no compass text, no watermark, no UI, no characters or portraits. Avoid mirrored symmetry and avoid a clean vertical division.
